Football.title: Leeds 3 Norwich 0: Watch Sean Longstaff’s worldie as he helps fire Whites through in FA Cup
Football.content:
LEEDS remain in the hunt for FA Cup glory after dumping out Norwich City.

The Premier League outfit avoided any sort of fifth round upset – unlike fellow top flight rivals Fulham and Sunderland – as they got the job done in the first half at Elland Road.





Philippe Clement has made an impact since joining the Canaries in the second tier, but he couldn’t find that magic touch to produce an upset.

Daniel Farke’s men thought they had the lead just before the 20 minute mark but Lukas Nmecha’s effort was ruled out by VAR after it spotted a handball in the build-up.

But just over 10 minutes later they grabbed the opener and there was no doubt at all over it after a stunner from Sean Longstaff.

It was a classy touch, turn and finish from the midfielder as he fired his effort beyond Daniel Grimshaw in the visiting goal.

11 minutes later, it was two.

Gabriel Gudmundsson reacted quickest in the box to a loose ball and slammed home to spark celebrations in the stands and on the park.

With five minutes left on the clock, they put the tie out of sight as Joel Piroe curled the ball home superbly as Leeds turned defence into attack in quick time.

Leeds had further chances to increase the gap further, while the visitors had an opportunity for a consolation but neither could convert.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Victory means Leeds are now in the hat for the quarter-final draw as they take another step towards FA Cup glory.

Focus will now naturally shift back to the Premier League survival fight with Crystal Palace next on the agenda.

For Norwich and Clement, it is back to Championship duty on Wednesday night at Carrow Road against Sheffield United before another home game next weekend against Preston.

Football.title: Inside Neil Warnock’s crazy unpaid return to management as 77-year-old fumes at referee in first game back
Football.content:
NEIL WARNOCK is back in the dugout and back on the richter scale.

At 77, the man with more promotions than a supermarket shelf has answered a desperate SOS from Torquay United — the club he credits for saving his managerial career 33 years ago.





On Saturday, the veteran— who boasts a record EIGHT promotions as a manager over 46 years— took charge of his 1,628th match, another all-time high in the game.

And despite a bruising 3-0 defeat in the National League South at Ebbsfleet, the fire in his belly is still raging.

When Torquay midfielder Matt Worthington got sent off for a mistimed tackle on Jake Hessenthaler early in the second half, Warnock was fuming with ref Kai Morgan, claiming he was being influenced by Ebbsfleet.

“They’re running the f***ing game,” he yelled at the official so loudly the entire stadium heard.



His loyal long-standing assistant Ronnie Jepson joined him in the dugout and barked: “You don’t know the f***ing game ref.”

But even Warnock had to calm down the big man — saying: “Forget him, Ronnie — it’s not worth it.”

And later he said: “The ref let 10 challenges go earlier worse than that.”

Warnock took caretaker charge of last Tuesday’s 2-2 draw at Farnborough after the Gulls sacked highly-popular boss Paul Wotton.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

And he says he will only be in dugout for the two upcoming home games against Salisbury tomorrow and Chesham on Saturday before handing over the reins to a new manager.

Warnock scaled the game from winning his first promotion by taking Scarborough into the EFL in 1987 to winning promotion to the top flight with the likes of Notts County, Sheffield United, QPR and Cardiff.

And yet here he was managing a team in the sixth tier that has not won for seven games — losing five of them.

Why? Because Torquay pulls at his heart-strings for a brief stint he had there in 1993.

He told me: “After taking Notts County up two divisions to the top flight, I turned down Chelsea out of loyalty — but within six months I got the sack.



“It killed me at the time and was thinking of packing it all in.

“But Kevin Blackwell, who was my goalkeeper at Scarborough and had been with me at County, talked me into going to Torquay for their last 14 games.

“We had some good offers at the time — Derby was one of them.



“But my wife Sharron and I went down to Torquay and we enjoyed it so much.

“They were going to get relegated but we managed to keep them in the Football League by winning at Carlisle in the penultimate game.

“We had players like Darren Moore and Paul Trollope going through brick walls for me for £50 a week.

“It gave me that belief again. I enjoyed motivating them and keeping them up.”

And it was while down in Devon that he visited Cornwall with Sharron on his days off — and the couple fell in love with the place and bought their forever home.



Warnock went on to grab two promotions with Plymouth but when his career took him to all parts of the country they kept on their farmhouse and rented wherever they went.

After a brief stint at Aberdeen two year ago, Warnock said he was finally calling it a day — but was helping Torquay out as an advisor.

But he said: “Last week they decided to part company with Paul and they literally had no-one who could take the team.



“They wanted me to do it until the end of the season but had to turn it down because I had other commitments. So I told them I’d do it for a maximum of four games.

“The club has a shortlist that has been whittled down and I’m helping them in that process.

“I like this league and the clubs in it. It’s not like I think this is beneath me. I started all those years ago in non-league with Gainsborough and was at Burton and Scarborough.

“I’m not getting paid. For me it’s nice to give something back and help a club who got me on the right path again.

“Torquay is like a throwback to the old-days. You don’t see Man United and Liverpool scarves down there. It’s all yellow and navy blue. We get crowds of between 3,000-5,000 — which is incredible for a team in the sixth tier.



“Although I’ve enjoyed it, I don’t like the fact we only had THREE outfield players on the bench and have an horrendous injury list — all key players.

“But we think some of them will be back next week and hopefully the club can still get a play-off spot.”

The Gulls currently sit sixth in the table and need to finish in the top seven to qualify. However, eight-placed Chelmsford are only two points behind with three games in hand.



Old school managers like Warnock seem to be going out of vogue.

But when you see a club like Tottenham in serious peril, you do wonder whether they should pick up phone to him.

Often teams in these situations just need someone who can generate a good changing room environment — his trademark.

Warnock said: “It wasn’t Thomas Frank’s fault. You can tell that the dressing room’s not good. I wouldn’t be surprised if it’s toxic in there.

“And you can’t do anything without a dressing room.



“I don’t know how somebody from abroad like the lad Igor Tudor who’s doing it, I don’t know how they’re going to get that changed, if I’m honest.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“But they’ll have to do it, or they’ll be in the Championship next year, won’t they?”

If that happens the hierarchy at N17 could do a lost worse than lure him Warnock out of retirement AGAIN.

Football.title: Man Utd ‘draw up stunning five-man shortlist to replace Marcus Rashford including PSG ace and Premier League rival’
Football.content:
MARCUS RASHFORD looks to have played his last game for Manchester United after a positive season on loan at Barcelona.

The England star was cast aside at Old Trafford following a fall-out with Ruben Amorim last year.



He has scored ten goals and provided nine assists in all competitions and Barca are now “convinced” about triggering their £25million option to buy, according to Sport.



The Spanish outlet now believes there are five players United could look at to replace Rashford next season.

Bradley Barcola

Being part of PSG’s Champions League-winning squad has done Barcola the world of good and he’s thriving under Luis Enrique.

The 23-year-old has ten goals and four assists overall and continues to earn a reputation as one of football’s rising talents.

He has 10 goals in Ligue 1 this season, with four assists in all competitions.

Sport calls him “one of the most promising wingers in European football” and “the most high profile” name on United’s shortlist.

Yan Diomande

The 19-year-old RB Leipzig star is enjoying a terrific individual season on the wing.



He has 11 goals and five assists in all competitions from 21 starts and is turning heads among Europe’s elite.

The Ivory Coast international is said to have impressed United chiefs with his “speed and dribbling ability”.

Jesus Rodriguez

United are also “keen” on Como star Rodriguez, the 20-year-old Spaniard who is going from strength to strength under Cesc Fabregas.



Rodriguez has 15 starts in Serie A this term and repaid his manager’s faith with seven assists.

In all competitions, he has three goals after scoring and assisting in Como’s 3-1 win over Lecce at the end of last month.

Martial Godo

United have already placed their trust in one young talent called Martial in years gone by.

And there could be a new exciting prospect in France with 22-year-old Strasbourg star Godo generating interest from United.



The forward has 11 goals and one assist in all competitions having been signed by Liam Rosenior before he left for Chelsea.

The English-born star was previously on the books at Fulham and plays for Ivory Coast internationally.

Iliman Ndiaye

It’s not been the most prolific season at Everton for Ndiaye but Sport claim he has done enough to gain attention from the Red Devils.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The Senegal international has six goals and three assists in all competitions for the Toffees after becoming a key man for David Moyes this season.



He has started 23 of Everton’s 29 Premier League games and is valued at around £40m by Transfermarkt.

Football.title: Bizarre football league table sees more than HALF teams start season on negative points after massive scandal
Football.content:
NINE teams began on minus points as the new Chinese Super League season kicked off this weekend.

The Chinese Football Association handed out heavy sanctions amid a comprehensive corruption investigation.



Tianjin Jinmen Tiger and last season’s runners-up Shanghai Shenhua were handed the biggest penalties, being deducted 10 points and fined one million yuan (£104,000) respectively.



A Chinese FA statement said that the investigation took into account “match-fixing, gambling, and corruption in the football industry”.

Thirteen clubs were punished as part of the probe, with four of these teams having already been relegated to China League One.

In addition to clubs being punished, 75 officials have been handed lifetime football bans.

These include former Everton midfielder and Chinese national team manager Li Tie.

In December 2024, Li, 48, was sentenced to 20 years in prison after confessing to giving and receiving more than £12.6million in bribes, while also helping to fix matches.

Chen Xuyuan, who served as president of China’s Football Association between 2019 and 2023 has also been banned for life.

The 69-year-old is serving a life sentence in prison having pleaded guilty in 2024 to taking bribes worth $11.2million (£8.3m).

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK



The Chinese Super League experienced an incredible financial boom during the 2010s, with most of the clubs backed by financial conglomerates.

High-profile stars including the likes of Oscar, Hulk, Marouane Fellaini and Marko Arnautovic joined the league.

The period of lavish spending ended in 2020, however, when the Chinese FA introduced new salary caps.

Under those rules, foreign player salaries were capped at £2.7million per year – around £52,000 per week.



Several teams also had to change their names ahead of the 2021 season, with clubs told to remove sponsors from their official titles.

The Chinese Super League now looks very different – with former stalwarts Guangzhou Evergrande, Hebei China Fortune, Jiangsu Suning, Dalian Yifang and Guangzhou R&F all having folded in recent years.

There are now hardly any recognisable names for English football fans, with Oscar the last big star to depart when he bid Shanghai a tearful farewell at the end of the 2024 season.

Former Arsenal midfielder Nico Yennaris – known by his Chinese name Li Ke – plays for Shanghai Shenhua.

While ex-Everton and England Under-21 defender Tyias Browning – Chinese name Jiang Guangtai – is a stalwart for Shanghai Port and the China national team.

Football.title: Arsenal star’s little-known manager dad makes history with stunning invincible season as he romps to title
Football.content:
MARTIN ODEGAARD’s dad, Hans Erik, has gone 41 games unbeaten at Norwegian side Lillestrom, earning promotion along the way.

Hired in January last year, Odegaard Sr has won a staggering 36 matches since taking over.





In that time his side have stormed the second division, winning the title last year as invincible champions and ending the season 25 POINTS ahead of their nearest challengers.

In 2025, Lillestrom only conceded 18 league goals in their 30 games, winning 25 and drawing just five.

It is the first time in the league’s history that a team has gone undefeated.

Lillestrom scored 87 times and had almost triple the goal difference of runners up Start.



It’s the second top-level senior position for Odegaard, 52, who also managed 131 matches at Sandefjord, where he spent three years as a player in the mid-2000s before retiring.

Having worked as an assistant and youth coach – including at Real Madrid, where son Martin moved in 2015 – he has impressed in his homeland.

Hans Erik was a player at Stromsgodset – Martin’s first club 20 years later – but is now making his name as a coach.

The Eliteserien season does not begin until next weekend with Lillestrom back in the top flight after just one year away.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Odegaard ensured an immediate return to the first division after relegation in 2023-24.

As well as lifting the NM-Cup at the end of December they are currently on a run of 23 straight wins.

Amazingly, Odegaard’s first match was actually a 2-2 draw but he has since gone on to dominate all before him.

Among his results are a 5-0, 6-0, 4-1, 7-1, 4-0 (twice), and 5-1 (x3) win.

They go to Aalesund in the first game of the new campaign before hosting Kristiansund the week after.



Viking are the title holders with Champions League history-makers Bodo/Glimt aiming to overthrow them.

Football.title: Man Utd legend Sir Alex Ferguson spotted at EFL clash with former Man City rival
Football.content:
SIR ALEX FERGUSON was spotted taking in an EFL thriller alongside an old rival as Bolton produced a stunning late comeback.

The Manchester United legend sat with former ManCity and Leeds manager Peter Reid.





Iconic duo Fergie, 84, and Reid, 69, were seen together in the stands as Bolton hosted Wycombe in League One.

Fans at the Toughsheet Community Stadium were treated to a dramatic finish worthy of “Fergie Time”.

Bolton looked dead and buried after falling 2-0 behind, but the hosts launched an incredible late rally.

Ruben Rodrigues sparked hope in the 88th minute to make it 2-1.



Then Mason Burstow levelled things up in the 91st minute.

The comeback was completed in sensational fashion when Corey Blackett-Taylor, 28, fired home the winner in the 95th minute.

Three goals in seven minutes sealed a memorable 3-2 victory for the promotion-chasing Trotters, who sit third in League One.

Ferguson and Reid share a long-standing relationship built on rivalry and respect.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Reid served as player-manager of Man City between 1990 and 1993 — regularly going head-to-head with Sir Alex.

The former midfielder even finished above United in the table during his first season in charge in 1990/91.

Reid later joked that managing City against Ferguson was “like fighting Mike Tyson with one hand behind your back.”

Of course, Ferguson won 13 Premier League titles whilst in charge of the Red Devils.



Despite the rivalry, the pair have remained close over the years and Fergie even wrote the foreword for Reid’s 2017 autobiography “Cheer Up”.

The match also had special meaning for Reid, who began his professional career with Bolton in 1974.

He made 273 appearances for the club before moving to Everton, where he became a key part of Howard Kendall’s legendary side.



Reid helped the Toffees win the FA Cup in 1984 and First Division titles in 1985 and 1987.

He also earned 13 caps for England, including playing in the infamous 1986 FIFA World Cup clash against Argentina starring Diego Maradona.

On that day at a sun-drenched Estadio Azteca, Maradona scored both the controversial “Hand of God” and the stunning “Goal of the Century.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Ferguson was also present at the 1986 World Cup in charge of Scotland.

But on this occasion, Reid and Ferguson were simply fans enjoying a thriller – and a comeback that would have made the United legend proud.

Football.title: Wild moment hundreds of Rangers and Celtic fans storm onto Ibrox pitch to confront each other and lob FIREWORKS
Football.content:
THE Scottish Cup quarter-final clash at Ibrox ended in chaos at full-time as fans from both sides invaded the pitch after Celtic’s penalty shootout win.

Tomas Cvancara’s winner sparked bedlam inside Ibrox as Celtic fans spilled onto the pitch in celebration before Rangers supporters attempted to confront their rivals.





Objects were thrown and fireworks were launched from both sets of supporters as ugly scenes marred the Scottish Cup tie.

Hundreds of fans were on the pitch and a Rangers supporter was seen being huckled away by cops after attempting to get near Celtic players and fans.

Stewards and police intervened to separate the two groups of fans, but players and staff members were caught up in the chaos.

A member of Celtic’s staff was allegedly attacked by a Gers supporter.

READ MORE FOOTBALL STORIES FAN DRAMA Shock moment group of ticketless Celtic fans charge through Ibrox door



Speaking live on TV, Premier Sports commentator Rory Hamilton described the shock incident, saying: “One of the Celtic coaching staff has been attacked by an individual.

“It is an ugly, ugly end to what has been a superb game of football. There is absolutely no need. This is lunacy.”

The SFA confirmed an investigation will be launched into the shocking full-time scenes.

A statement read: “The Scottish FA condemns the behaviour from supporters entering the field of play following today’s Scottish Gas Scottish Cup Quarter-Final at Ibrox Stadium.



OUR BEST FREE BETS & SIGN-UP OFFERS

“An investigation will be carried out immediately in line with the Judicial Panel Protocol.”

This was the first derby since 2018 that Celtic had the full Broomloan Stand at Ibrox.

Celtic had just 2,000 supporters at Ibrox last weekend for their Premiership showdown, but 7,500 away fans were allowed in for this Scottish Cup tie.

Scottish Cup rules state that away teams are entitled to a 20 per cent ticket allocation, so the Hoops were given the increased share for the derby clash.

It was hoped the one-off exception could potentially lead to a permanent return of a full away allocation for Old Firm matches.



However, both sets of fans have done themselves no favours in trying to prove that the larger allocation could return to the derby fixture.

One fan was left on the ground and covered in blood during the pitch invasion.

Police and stewards eventually restored order after masked Rangers fans rushed from the Copland end to confront their rivals at the opposite side of the stadium.

But chaos continued outside of the stadium as riot cops raced to the area.

Scores of mounted cops on horseback came galloping into the area outside the Broomloan Stand amid the post game chaos.



Riot vans came speeding into the stadium footprint with officers donning helmets amid the chaotic scenes.

Some Celtic supporters outside the ground began chanting as they learned the result of the match.

A Buckfast bottle was then thrown over a police barricade by Rangers supporters.

Once the chaos was quickly quelled a horde of jubilant Hoops fans came pouring out of the Broomloan Stand.

There were chats of “Mon the Hoops” and other songs as they celebrated the penalty win.



Some supporters even sang “they’re going to cut the allocation” as a cheeky swipe to SFA chiefs who look set to slash the number of away fans allowed at Scottish Cup matches following the pitch invasion.

Others waved Celtic flags while some even held up pieces of broken seats stolen from the stand.

Footage shows bottles being launched from Gers fans towards the Broomloan Stand, while broken seats were thrown from the opposite direction.

It came amid furious tensions with cops before the start of the game.

Celtic supporters were held at the bottom of Broomloan Road and funnelled between two rows of police vans.



Police horses were also drafted in.

Tensions boiled over and a number of supporters charged past cops and security staff.

Police blamed the chaos on ticketless fans and Celtic supporters gathering at the bottom of the entryway.

Angered supporters hurled abuse at officers and branded the chaos as “disgraceful”.



Some young supporters were left in tears and had to be consoled by their parents after being caught up in the blockage. Others were seen limping away after being hurt.

Scottish Conservative MSP for Glasgow Annie Wells said: “These disgraceful scenes should have no place at a match, however intense the rivalry.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“The police are already stretched to the limit thanks to SNP cuts and should not have to deal with this kind of thuggish behaviour when policing a game.”

Football.title: Referee left awkwardly staring at blank screen in penalty chaos after football ultra unplugs pitchside VAR monitor
Football.content:
A REFEREE was left awkwardly staring at a BLANK SCREEN after a cheeky ultra unplugged the VAR monitor.

The hapless whistler was officiating the German second division clash between Munster and Hertha Berlin.





He had been called to the screen to review a penalty for Hertha after initially waving away protests.

But as he looked for a replay, there were NO images or clips of the incident because the screen was totally blank.

That’s after a balaclava-wearing Munster ultra had sneakily UNPLUGGED the monitor to sabotage the check.

And he must have been egged on by the rest of the crowd, with a banner reading: “Pull the plug on VAR.”

A photo in German outlet BILD shows the alleged culprit leaning over to tamper with the tech.

It meant was referee Felix Bickel couldn’t make the call over the penalty.

So the VAR assistant in the Cologne video centre stepped in to make the decision instead.

The audio, that was announced over the stadium PA system was heard saying: “We reviewed a possible foul in the penalty area.



“My colleague Katrin Rafalski determined that Munster’s number 24 clearly made contact with [Michael] Cuisance’s shin.

“Therefore, my final decision is: penalty kick.”

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK



It was a game full of twists, turns and drama throughout all the way up to the final seconds.

Fabian Reese opened the scoring for Hertha in first half stoppage time from 12-yards before hosts Munster levelled in the opening minute of the second-half.

Marten Winkler – off the bench for Hertha – claimed a dramatic 93rd minute winner to spark wild scenes for the away side both on the park and in the stands.

The three points has Hertha sitting sixth in the 2.Bundesliga standings – 10 points behind leaders Schalke.

Munster remain down the bottom end in 14th, just three points ahead of bottom club Magdeburg.

Football.title: Gareth Bale gives Zinedine Zidane insight after Real Madrid icon linked to Man Utd job
Football.content:
Gareth Bale gives Zinedine Zidane insight after Real Madrid icon linked to Man Utd job

Gareth Bale has given an insight into Zinedine Zidane's coaching methods at Real Madrid as the Frenchman continues to be linked with the Manchester United manager's job

View 2 Images Gareth Bale worked with Zinedine Zidane at Real Madrid. (Image: Soccrates Images, Getty Images)

Former Real Madrid star Gareth Bale has offered a glimpse into Zinedine Zidane's coaching approach amid ongoing speculation linking him with Manchester United. Several potential contenders have been linked with the position at Old Trafford, with Michael Carrick in interim control until the season's end.

‌



Carrick was brought in during January on a temporary arrangement, but may be considered for the role permanently if he maintains his remarkable run of results for United. Prior to United's loss to Newcastle earlier this week, the team had secured six victories in seven fixtures, collecting 19 points from a potential 21 available in the Premier League, which has seen the club rise to third position in the standings.

‌



Despite the positive sequence, various contenders remain connected with the United manager's position permanently from the summer, with Zidane referenced regularly throughout recent years.

‌



Man Utd's unsung hero who has helped Michael Carrick in area he and Ruben Amorim differ on READ MORE :

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Zidane has been without employment since departing Real Madrid in 2021, and has been consistently associated with a comeback to management ever since, with United and the France national side mentioned as prospective destinations.

Bale featured under Zidane throughout his time with Los Blancos, securing five Champions League crowns, three of which came under Zidane's stewardship. Discussing Zidane's tenure as boss at the Santiago Bernabeu, Bale offered a glimpse into what it was like working under the 53-year-old, especially during training sessions.

‌



"Zidane as a coach? Honestly, he didn't do much," Bale said on the Stick to Football podcast.

"We'd go over some tactics before matches against Barcelona or Bayern-15 minutes of defensive work. Other than that, it was just possession games, shooting drills, and then we'd go home.

View 2 Images Gareth Bale has given an insight into what it is like to work with Zinedine Zidane. (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

‌



"But he earned respect thanks to his qualities as a player. He participated in training, even beat me a few times – he's still in great shape."

United will resume Premier League duties next weekend when they host Aston Villa at Old Trafford, having enjoyed a break this week owing to their absence from the FA Cup.

The Reds will then travel to Bournemouth on March 22, their final fixture before the international break, and won't feature again until April 13 when they host rivals Leeds United at Old Trafford.

Football.title: Celtic boss Martin O'Neill responds to Rangers pitch invasion that forced police to step in
Football.content:
Celtic boss Martin O'Neill responds to Rangers pitch invasion that forced police to step in

Celtic’s Scottish Cup victory against fierce rivals Rangers was marred by chaotic scenes as fans flooded the pitch shortly after the final whistle with both managers having now spoken out

View 2 Images Ugly scenes marred Celtic's Scottish Cup win vs Rangers (Image: Steve Welsh/PA Wire)

Martin O’Neill admitted his concern that an angry pitch invasion could potentially taint Celtic’s Scottish Cup victory against Rangers, while rival boss Danny Rohl condemned the scenes.

‌



The Hoops beat their Old Firm rivals on penalties on Sunday afternoon. The game signalled the first occasion in recent seasons that Celtic were permitted to have their full complement of away fans at Ibrox - but things unfortunately turned ugly at the final whistle.

‌



Some Celtic fans spilled onto the pitch, with Rangers supporters responding in kind. They made their way over to rival fans, with missiles including pyrotechnics clearly thrown.

‌



Speaking in his post-match press conference, Celtic boss O’Neill gave his thoughts.. He said: “It was a big derby game. I think there was a lot of jubilation at us winning.

“Obviously the fans spilt over. I suppose it’s very difficult to quell that if that’s the case. Maybe it should be the first question, maybe it shouldn’t be. I thought it might be your third question.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Rangers fans storm onto pitch after Celtic penalty shootout in chaotic Old Firm scenes READ MORE :

Football fans clash in violent scenes as police forced to use tasers in huge brawl READ MORE :

"I just thought the team were absolutely fantastic. Fourth game in ten days. Absolutely out on our feet. We defended magnificently for long periods of the game. Thankfully we came through.”

Asked if his staff and players were safe, he added: “As we were making our way back, there was some sort of fracass. Somebody tried to get on the field. I don’t know, all I saw was there was a bit of a scrum down at the bottom.

"As you say, maybe it does taint proceedings. I think there was an element of self protection in that one, if that’s the case. I’m making judgments here on things that I only half saw.

‌



“From my viewpoint, it was a great, great win for us. We were under pressure during the course of the game, as you would expect Rangers are very strong. They’ve had the full week to go through. We’ve had to travel to Aberdeen, our fourth away game in ten days. Just fantastic effort by the players, really, really fantastic.”

View 2 Images Rangers Ultras invade the pitch following the Scottish Gas Scottish Cup Quarter Final match between Rangers and Celtic (Image: Ian MacNicol/Getty Images)

O’Neill then said: “I think that would be a worry [tainted the game]. For me, from a distance, I’ve been saying that the fixture itself, although still a phenomenal fixture there’s no question about that, lost a bit of its lustre because of the away fans, the full compliment of away fans weren’t allowed in.

‌



“I have no idea what might happen. From that viewpoint yes. I think today, with our crowd in there, to me it was really a proper Old Firm game in that sense. We’ll see what develops.”

Rangers boss Danny Rohl has also spoken out on the scenes. He said: “I’m honest, I was not on the pitch in this moment. I didn’t see it until now. Just that something was on the pitch which was the case.

“I think we all know all of the emotion of the situation after a game. Nobody likes to see this. I think it was a great atmosphere for 120 minutes. I think it should be like this. All the other things should not be in the stadium or in football.”

Football.title: Declan Rice reveals what sets Arsenal apart from Premier League title rivals
Football.content:
Declan Rice reveals what sets Arsenal apart from Premier League title rivals

Arsenal are currently the favourites to claim this year’s Premier League title under Mikel Arteta which would see them finally secure the trophy after years of near-misses

View 2 Images Declan Rice has shared his verdict on Arsenal's title chances (Image: Getty Images)

Declan Rice claims that Arsenal’s lack of long-term injuries has seen them move ahead of their rivals in the race for the Premier League title. The Gunners are currently in prime position to lift the trophy come the end of the season.

‌



Arsenal sit seven points clear of nearest rivals Manchester City with eight games to be played. Should they go all the way, it would see the club end their over two-decade wait for a league title, while it would see them finally get over the line having been pipped in each of the last two campaigns.

‌



Midfielder Rice has suggested that Arsenal’s lack of injuries has seen them put themselves in control. Speaking to Hayters, he said: “Last year, the injuries killed us.

‌



"I know a lot of teams this year have been having the same problem, so I can't complain about that, but we lost a lot of key players last year. We have an amazing squad this year. Players you can rely on in every position.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal suffer new injury blow as attacker forced off vs Mansfield in FA Cup battle READ MORE :

“Also, there’s a calmness. You go in every day, you see the boys. It’s not all serious. It’s calm, it’s relaxed.

‌



"It's like a family. You're going in, everyone’s in their early 20s, no one’s really above 30. Everyone's vibing. It's a nice group to be around.

"We have such a good set of staff all around the building, every department. So everything’s been really good from start to start to now. We’re getting into the business end, which is the most important part now."

Arsenal have of course had to deal with a few issues this season; Kai Havertz and Martin Odegaard, Mikel Merino and William Saliba all among those to have had lengthy periods on the sidelines.

‌



View 2 Images Rice and Gabriel have been key to Arsenal this season (Image: Getty Images)

And that saw boss Mikel Arteta make a number of changes to his side for the FA Cup fifth round clash with Mansfield Town.

"Well, they had issues and they've been carrying issues," Arteta said when asked to explain his selection decisions. "And the amount of games that we are playing, it's obviously super demanding.

‌



"It was the only time to try to make sure that we can clean those issues that they're having to start the next sequence of two weeks before the break in the best possible way. And we had to make those decisions."

Arsenal turn their attention to the Champions League on Wednesday night with a clash with German side Bayer Leverkusen. They will then be aiming to continue their push for the title as they take on Everton.

Football.title: Masked fan unplugs VAR during match as club release statement
Football.content:
Masked fan unplugs VAR during match as club release statement

One fan took it into their own hands to try and end the use of VAR during a football match in Germany and they certainly caused an interruption through their masked escapades

View 2 Images The VAR screen was not working (Image: Sky)

A masked vigilante fan unplugged a VAR screen in protest of the technology's use during a match in Germany. When referee Felix Bickel was called to the sidelines to review a penalty decision during Preussen Munster vs Hertha Berlin, he was unable to see a replay.

‌



After initially not awarding a spot-kick when Munster's Niko Koulis brought down Michael Cuisance, Bickel was advised to his VAR to take another look, but couldn't. A Munster ultra had unplugged the monitor in a bid to sabotage the use of technology.

‌



The action lined up with a banner in the stands that read: "Pull the plug on VAR." The saboteur was not spotted in action, but a supporter wearing a green morph-like face covering and overalls that would not go amiss at a crime scene investigation was seen being helped back into the stands.

‌



For opponents of VAR, it was bad news, however, as the technology was still able to be used. Video referee Katrin Rafalski ultimately took the decision from a remote location, in the VAR room in Cologne.

The club home side have said they are investigating the incidents and are putting actions in place to avoid a repeat of the situation.

Rangers fans storm onto pitch after Celtic penalty shootout in chaotic Old Firm scenes READ MORE :

Newcastle fan rubs salt into wounds with Alan Shearer tribute after Sunderland goal READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



A statement from Preussen Munster read: "In first-half stoppage time between SC Preußen Münster and Hertha BSC, VAR intervened after a controversial incident in the penalty area.

However, the referee was unable to review the incident using the VAR monitor in the stadium because a masked spectator from the active fan section illegally entered the pitch and unplugged the technical equipment.

"The penalty decision was ultimately made by video referee Katrin Rafalski in the VAR room in Cologne.

‌



View 2 Images Fans of SC Preussen Münster wave flags during the 2. Bundesliga match between SC Preußen Münster and Hertha BSC at Preussenstadion (Image: Christof Koepsel/Getty Images)

"SC Preußen Münster regrets the incident and will do everything in its power to identify and bring the perpetrator(s) to justice. Furthermore, immediate measures have been taken to prevent similar incidents in the future.

"Initial findings indicate that this was a planned action – a banner to that effect was displayed in the home section shortly after the technical malfunction."

Article continues below

Munster ultimately lost the Bundesliga 2 match 2-1 after Hertha netted a 93rd-minute goal through Martin Winkler. The away side had taken the lead via the penalty in the opening half as Fabian Reese scored from the spot but Jannis Heuer found an equaliser in the second period.

Football.title: Port Vale hero Ben Waine can't hide true feelings after huge Sunderland FA Cup shock
Football.content:
Port Vale hero Ben Waine can't hide true feelings after huge Sunderland FA Cup shock

Port Vale striker Ben Waine was the hero in their stunning FA Cup triumph over Sunderland - but the Newcastle fan will not be able to meet his boyhood club in the next round

View 2 Images Ben Waine and Jon Brady were all smiles after dumping Sunderland out of the FA Cup (Image: James Gill/Danehouse)

Newcastle fan Ben Waine dumped Sunderland out of the FA Cup and then admitted it was beyond his wildest dreams.

‌



Port Vale hero Waine, 24, scored the winner to stun Sunderland in the biggest upset of the round and even marked his goal by copying Newcastle great Alan Shearer’s trademark celebration.

‌



Waine was born in New Zealand, his mum Jacque is a Geordie which is why he grew up supporting Newcastle and he enjoyed his greatest day by knocking out the Premier League giants.

‌



New Zealand international Waine said: “It doesn't get better than that. That was something I've never even dreamt of, for it to go that way I think my family will be happy!

OPINION John Cross Port Vale STUN Sunderland in huge FA Cup upset as Newcastle fan Ben Waine strikes again

Newcastle fan rubs salt into wounds with Alan Shearer tribute after Sunderland goal READ MORE :

“It's unbelievable, look at this place. I've never seen it like this. We dug in so hard, I think we deserved it. We stuck to the game plan - it's as simple as that. Hands down the biggest upset I’ve been involved in, yeah. It's unbelievable.”

‌



Waine says he is dreaming of going to the World Cup with New Zealand but was just disappointed that he cannot play Newcastle after they were knocked out by Manchester City.

“Not to get too ahead of myself, but that's obviously the dream, yeah,” he said. “I was gonna say, Newcastle so honestly, I don't know. Whoever it is will take it to them, hopefully, and be another great game.”

Waine has been Port Vale’s match winner in three FA Cup ties this season as the club rooted to the foot of League One have picked up under new boss Jon Brady who has been in charge for 14 games.

‌



View 2 Images Waine was Port Vale's FA Cup hero again vs Sunderland as he fired the League One side into the quarter-finals (Image: Gareth Copley)

But Brady admitted the cup run can feel like an unwanted distraction. “It’s a bit of a pain in the bum, to be honest,” said Brady.

“It is really because what it’s doing for us is adding to our fixture list and as you saw we list one of our best centre halves and we haven’t got the size of the squad to cope with the amount of games at the moment.

‌



“I have not got a clue who is left! I saw Arsenal win their game yesterday, saw Chelsea just edge Wrexham and Man City, Liverpool … my God. I wouldn’t have a clue because I’m thinking about Bradford on Wednesday.”

Sunderland boss Regis Le Bris has a raft of injuries but fielded his strongest available line-up and said: “We went strong with the ambition to win, with the knowledge as well, that it was a tough challenge because of the challenge here, with the crowd, the team fully involved, full credit them.

“The pitch is the pitch. We won't find excuses. It levels everything, technically and tactically. You put in the battle and fighting spirit and they were good at that, the crowd the stadium the event. They deserved on that part of the game to win."

Football.title: Newcastle fan rubs salt into wounds with Alan Shearer tribute after Sunderland goal
Football.content:
Newcastle fan rubs salt into wounds with Alan Shearer tribute after Sunderland goal

Port Vale striker Ben Waine scored the only goal as his side beat Sunderland 1-0 in the FA Cup fifth round, celebrating with Alan Shearer's iconic tribute

View 2 Images Ben Waine of Port Vale celebrates after scoring against Sunderland (Image: Nick Potts/PA Wire)

Port Vale striker Ben Waine honoured Newcastle United icon Alan Shearer with his goal celebration during the match against Sunderland. The Black Cats were knocked out of the FA Cup to the League One side, suffering a 1-0 loss.

‌



Sunderland fell behind in the first half when Waine broke the deadlock for Port Vale in the 28th minute at Vale Park.

‌



Waine made the switch to Port Vale last summer from Plymouth Argyle and had previously spent a season on loan at Mansfield Town, having started his professional career in his native New Zealand.

‌



The striker nodded the ball into the net following a scramble in the six-yard box with Sunderland's defenders, before racing away in celebration.

Forest Green Rovers boss Robbie Savage was swift to praise Waine after the goal. “It’s a great header, he [Waine] had to generate the power himself,” said Savage while on commentary for TNT Sports.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



“Finger-tips from the goalkeeper, but they [Port Vale] had first, second and third contact, and it’s a brilliant header.

“Look how much power he has to generate, and he loops it. 1-0 Port Vale.”

Waine's celebrations saw him sprint past the away Sunderland supporters before linking up with his colleagues to mark the goal in front of the home crowd at Vale Park.

‌



As Waine sprinted past the Sunderland supporters, the striker appeared to copy the iconic celebration made famous by Newcastle legend Shearer, who netted four times against the Black Cats throughout his playing days.

View 2 Images Ben Waine as Port Vale's FA Cup hero again vs Sunderland (Image: Gareth Copley)

A lifelong Newcastle supporter, Waine clearly relished finding the back of the net against his beloved club's fiercest rivals.

‌



The forward had also netted Port Vale's decisive strike in the preceding round against Bristol City during extra time, securing a 1-0 triumph for the League One outfit in their fourth-round tie.

Newcastle had already exited the FA Cup earlier that weekend following a 3-1 home defeat to Manchester City at St James' Park.

Sunderland were hoping to join Liverpool, Arsenal, Chelsea, Man City and Southampton in progressing to the FA Cup quarter-finals, but Waine's strike left them facing a considerable challenge in their clash with Port Vale.

Football.title: Iran footballers 'fear death penalty' after anthem protest and 'traitors' label
Football.content:
Iran footballers 'fear death penalty' after anthem protest and 'traitors' label

The Iranian women's team have been taking part in the Asian Cup in Australia, where they staged a protest.

View 2 Images Islamic Republic of Iran pose for a team photo during ahead of their clash against the Philippines. (Image: Albert Perez/Getty Images)

The Iranian women’s football team are reportedly fearing facing the death penalty for treason after staging a protest against the country’s current regime.

‌



The team have been in action in the Women’s Asian Cup in Australia. They staged their protest during their opening game against South Korea on Monday, when they refused to sing the national anthem and perform a military salute.

‌



They fell to a 3-0 loss in that game. They were back in action on Thursday when they fell to a 4-0 loss against hosts Australia, before exiting the tournament on Sunday with a 2-0 loss against the Philippines.

‌



Lincoln City fans tease Tottenham with new chant as Spurs face relegation threat READ MORE :

The games against Australia and the Philippines saw them sing the national anthem and perform the salute. However, the protest has led to Iranian state TV labelling them as ‘traitors’.

Presenter Mohammad Reza Shahbazi sent a warning following the protest: "In times of war, traitors must be dealt with more harshly," he said. "Anyone who takes even a single step against the country during wartime must face stronger consequences."

Their exit from the tournament means that the team are now due to travel back to Iran. However, there are now some fears for their safety following the protest.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Refugee Council chief executive Paul Power said: "Certainly on the evidence that's available, it would appear that the members of the women's soccer team are at risk if they're returned."

Journalist Ali Bornaei tagged the foreign minister Penny Wong on X, writing: "The lives of the Iranian Women's National Football Team are in imminent danger.

‌



"After their peaceful protest in Australia, Iranian state-linked media has officially labelled them 'wartime traitors'.

"In Iran, 'treason' is a capital offence punishable by death. These athletes face arbitrary detention and execution if forced to return."

View 2 Images The Iran Women's football team have been in Asian Cup action in Australia. (Image: Albert Perez/Getty Images)

‌



Australia's shadow attorney general, Julian Leeser has called on the Australian government to offer asylum to the team. "On International Women's Day we must stand up for the women in our midst, he said.

"We know that in recent days serious threats have been made against the courageous Iranian women's soccer team who are playing in Australia. Given the serious threats, members of the Iranian team should be offered asylum if they want it.

"The Australian government should not turn a blind eye to the danger these women face."

Article continues below

Several Iranian community organisations and civil society groups have also been in contact with the Australian government, outlining the concerns that they have for the team should they return to Iran.

Football.title: Football fans clash in violent scenes as police forced to use tasers in huge brawl
Football.content:
Football fans clash in violent scenes as police forced to use tasers in huge brawl

Chaos eruputed after Flint Town sealed their spot in the Welsh Cup semi-final with a 5-1 win over Bangor at the Go Goodwins Stadium in Llandudno with police having now responded

View 2 Images Violence broke out after the Welsh Cup clash between Flint Town and Bangor

Police have appealed for information after violence broke out following a Welsh Cup clash at the weekend. Flint Town sealed their semi-final spot in the competition with a 5-1 win against domestic rivals Bangor at the Go Goodwins Stadium in Llandudno.

‌



But scenes quickly turned ugly, with violence breaking out outside of a pub in the Welsh town. North Wales Police have confirmed that five people have been arrested.

‌



Footage shared to social media showed officers detaining a number of supporters, with one police officer pulling out a taser in the shocking scenes.

‌



A police statement read: "Following the Welsh Cup semi-final between Flint Town United and CPD Bangor 1876 on Saturday (March 7), officers were called at 3.59pm to an incident of serious disorder involving a large number of people outside the Nevill public house on Vaughan Street, Llandudno.

Liverpool and Man Utd act over sickening Grok posts mocking Hillsborough, Munich and Jota READ MORE :

"Five individuals were subsequently arrested on suspicion of public order offences, S.47 ABH and assaulting an emergency worker. This remains a live investigation including the reviewing of video footage and CCTV.

"We are aware of considerable interest on social media, including widespread sharing of recorded footage by people present, and we would ask anyone who recorded the disorder to send the video to us to assist in our ongoing investigations."

‌



A spokesperson for the for the Welsh FA (FAW) also responded to the incident. They said: "The JD Welsh Cup Semi-Final between Flint Town United and Bangor City 1876 at the Go Goodwins Stadium in Llandudno was played without incident.

View 2 Images An officer was forced to pull out a taser

"The FAW are aware of reports of alleged disorder in Llandudno town centre following the conclusion of the match, and whilst this falls outside of the FAW’s jurisdiction, the association will support the North Wales Police investigation if required. The JD Welsh Cup Semi-Final itself was a positive event for Welsh football. Violence has no place in football and damages the positive experience that is enjoyed by the majority."

‌



"CPD Bangor City 1876 has established policies and procedures to address any issues raised and will follow those processes should they be required," a statement from Bangor City said.

"We understand the matter is currently subject to ongoing action by North Wales Police, therefore, at this time, the club is unable to comment further.

"CPD Bangor City 1876 has established policies and procedures to address any issued raised and will follow those processes should they be required. Further updates will be provided if and when appropriate, until then the club will be making no further comments."

Football.title: Sivo hat-trick takes Leeds to Castleford victory as Hull KR bounce back
Football.content:
Maika Sivo scored a hat-trick as Leeds claimed a 22-4 Betfred Super League win over Castleford.

The winger's impressive contribution took his tally to nine tries in three games and secured three successive Super League wins for the Rhinos.

Castleford fell behind at Headingley when George Brown opened the scoring with his first Super League try and Jake Connor converted.

The visitors then replied when Darnell McIntosh quickly offloaded for Mikaele Ravalawa to cross in the corner, but Tom Weaver's subsequent conversion attempt flew wide.

Image: Sivo celebrates his second try against Castleford

Sivo opened his account in the 29th minute. Leeds claimed a 10-4 lead at half-time and Sivo extended their advantage further early in the second half from the wing, with Connor converting.

Rhinos charged ahead when Sivo scored his third of the game in the 55th minute, with Connor's kick also successful.

Leeds then saw out the win to climb into second in the table, two points behind leaders Wigan, while the Tigers sit in 10th.

Hull KR shrug off Vegas hangover

Hull KR shrugged off their Las Vegas nightmare and belatedly kick-started the defence of their Betfred Super League title with a 32-6 win at hapless Huddersfield.

Rovers were half a world away from last season's all-conquering treble-winning campaign when they crashed to a 58-6 loss to Leeds at the Allegiant Stadium last week.

They were effectively gifted their first points of the domestic year as Tom Davies and Mikey Lewis both crossed twice in a six-try show against the rock-bottom Giants.

Oliver Gildart served an ominous warning by scoring Rovers' opener with less than three minutes on the clock, and although the expected avalanche was not forthcoming, the visitors barely had to break sweat as they proceeded to wrap up victory.

Image: George Flanagan of Huddersfield fails to stop Tom Davies of Hull KR from scoring a try (Photo: Olly Hassell/SWpix.com)

Good spells of possession from the Giants, who added Sam Halsall and Zac Woolford to their injury list last week, were undermined by frequent errors and an inspired piece of playmaking as Tyrone May sent Davies over for Rovers' second on 28 minutes.

May was proving the game's star performer by some distance, and he served up Rovers' third in the 38th minute with a brilliant, looping ball to send Joe Burgess in on the left flank.

One conversion apiece from Lewis and Rhyse Martin bolstered Rovers' half-time advantage to 16 points, and they increased it within moments of the restart as Lewis made space and threw an outrageous dummy to wrongfoot Asher O'Donnell and trot over for his side's fourth.

Huddersfield broke through when the gutsy George Flanagan dropped onto Tui Lolohea's high kick under the posts. But Rovers responded with a sweeping, crossfield move that sent Davies in for his second.

May sent Lewis clattering over before Martin contributed his third and final successful kick.

Sky Sports will again show every game of the Super League live this season - including two matches in each round exclusively live, with the remaining five matches each week shown on Sky Sports+

Football.title: India hammer New Zealand in final to make T20 World Cup history
Football.content:
India became the first men's side to win the T20 World Cup three times and the first to claim back-to-back titles with an emphatic 96-run victory over New Zealand continuing their white-ball dominance.

India smoked 255-5 after losing the toss in Ahmedabad, with Sanju Samson (89 off 46 balls) passing fifty for the third straight match and opening partner Abhishek Sharma (52 off 21) slamming the fastest fifty of this year's tournament, from 18 deliveries.

New Zealand could muster only 159 all out in 19 overs in reply - they were 72-5 in 18.1 - as pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah (4-15) picked up each of his wickets with slower balls.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Watch the moment the last New Zealand wicket fell as India claimed back-to-back Men's T20 World Cup titles and a record third in total

Score summary - India beat New Zealand by 96 runs India 255-5 in 20 overs (put in to bat): Sanju Samson (89 off 46 balls), Ishan Kishan (54 off 25), Abhishek Sharma (52 off 21), Shivam Dube (26no off 8), Hardik Pandya (18 off 13); Jimmy Neesham (3-46) New Zealand 159 all out in 19 overs (target 256): Tim Seifert (52 off 26), Mitchell Santner (43 off 35); Jasprit Bumrah (4-15), Axar Patel (3-27)

Bumrah was on a hat-trick after cleaning up Jimmy Neesham (8) and Matt Henry (0) in the 16th over and ended with career-best T20 international figures after dismissing New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner (43 off 35).

Ishan Kishan - who had earlier tonked 54 from 25 balls - also produced two excellent catches, the second of which, a juggling effort at deep midwicket, removed Tim Seifert (52 off 26), and then a simple one to oust Daryl Mitchell (17).

Since going over a decade without picking up a global limited-overs title following Champions Trophy glory in England in 2013, India have won the last three, with their T20 triumphs in 2024 and now this year sandwiching the 2025 Champions Trophy.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player India's Sanju Samson smoked 89 from 46 balls as his side hammered New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final

India beat New Zealand in that 50-over final in Dubai a year ago and routed the same side on Sunday, becoming the first men's team to win the T20 World Cup at home and extending the Black Caps' wait for a maiden World Cup title after a fourth defeat in a final.

India get off to a flier in final

An early onslaught left the Kiwis shellshocked as Samson and Abhishek pounded India to 98-0 after seven overs - ample wides adding to the score and New Zealand's decision to select pacer Jacob Duffy (0-42) in place of off-spinner Cole McConchie spectacularly backfiring - while the hosts were then 203-1 after 15.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jimmy Neesham took three wickets in the 16th over of the final, removing Samson, Ishan Kishan and India skipper Suryakumar Yadav

Neesham's three-wicket burst in one over, in which Samson, Kishan and Suryakumar Yadav all fell - Suryakumar for a golden duck to a diving catch from Rachin Ravindra in the deep - dragged New Zealand back into the game, only for Shivam Dube (26no off 8) to collar Neesham's final over for 24 as India eclipsed the 253-7 they had piled on against England in the semi-final run-fest in Mumbai.

Dube dropped Finn Allen on nought in the first over of the chase, a blunder that could have cost India with the Kiwi opener blazing a T20 World Cup record 33-ball hundred in the nine-wicket demolition of South Africa in the last four.

However, Allen (9) holed out off Axar (3-27) Patel before Bumrah removed Ravindra (1) with his first delivery - a superb slower ball that Kishan caught on the dive at deep square - and India went on claim a first victory over New Zealand in four attempts at T20 World Cups following losses in 2007, 2016 and 2021.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player 'Magician' Jasprit Bumrah dismissed Rachin Ravindra from the first ball he bowled in Ahmedabad

India will aim to claim a first 50-over World Cup since the 2011 edition on home soil when the ODI showpiece is next held in Africa towards the end of 2027, having finished runners-up to Australia in 2023.

India banish Ahmedabad blues

Since that defeat in Ahmedabad three years ago, India have only suffered one loss across the next three ICC limited-overs events - earlier this month against South Africa in the T20 World Cup Super 8s, also in Ahmedabad.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Kishan's juggling catch removed New Zealand opener Tim Seifert for 52 off 26 deliveries

There would be no issues at that venue against New Zealand, though, with batting brutality, fielding excellence - bar Dube's early dose of butterfingers and some sloppy errors from Hardik Pandya - and genius Bumrah playing their part.

Bumrah's final wicket came when he castled Santner with a delicious off-cutter in the 18th over, before Abhishek picked up the title-winning wicket when Duffy (3) skied to long-on.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell was unhappy after Arshdeep Singh's throw towards the non-striker's stumps made contact with him

New Zealand's runners-up finish follows the same result in the 2015 and 2019 50-over World Cups and 2021 T20 World Cup.

They lost by the "barest of margins", to nick Ian Smith's iconic phrase, against England in an ODI World Cup epic seven years ago at Lord's, but this defeat at Narendra Modi Stadium was resounding.

Football.title: Crowd violence overshadows Celtic's Old Firm shoot-out win in Scottish Cup
Football.content:
Crowd trouble marred Celtic's dramatic penalty shoot-out victory over Old Firm rivals Rangers which secured them a spot in the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

Fans of both clubs spilled on to the pitch at full-time and launched missiles at one another as the police were forced to break up the violence.

This was the first time travelling supporters had been allowed the traditional away allocation of 7,500 tickets in eight years.

Despite failing to register a shot on target in 120 minutes of goalless football, last year's beaten finalists Celtic found a way to win again. Rangers captain James Tavernier hit the bar with the first penalty before fellow substitute Djeidi Gassama blazed over, with Tomas Cvancara then sealing the victory as Celtic ran out 4-2 winners from the spot before dozens of their fans invaded the pitch.

Image: The Celtic squad celebrates as Tomas Cvancara scores the winning penalty

That sparked an invasion from hundreds of Rangers supporters and missiles were thrown as police and stewards moved to form a barrier. Flares were thrown and some minor skirmishes appeared to take place before order was restored and the away fans were left to celebrate alone.

With league leaders Hearts, cup holders Aberdeen and fellow top-six sides Motherwell and Hibernian already out, Martin O'Neill's Celtic are now red-hot favourites to lift the trophy. They will learn their last-four opponents following the conclusion of St Mirren vs Partick Thistle this evening [7.30pm].

Danny Röhl's Rangers are now out of both domestic cup competitions and Europe, meaning the league title is their only remaining hope of lifting silverware.

How Celtic booked their semi-final spot

The crowd trouble started well before kick-off as ticketless Celtic supporters forced entry to the Broomloan Stand, causing Police Scotland to close the turnstiles for a short period of time.

On the pitch, the match started in similar to fashion to last week's league clash at Ibrox with the home side well on top.

Celtic were missing captain Callum McGregor and Kieran Tierney following injuries picked up in the midweek win at Aberdeen.

Luke McCowan came into central midfield, while Liam Scales moved to left-back as Auston Trusty returned amid a three-match league suspension. Daizen Maeda started through the middle.

Image: Youssef Chermiti challenges Hyun-Jun Yang

There was also a notable omission in the Rangers team as John Souttar was dropped to the bench along with Tochi Chukwuani.

The hosts forced the early pressure and there was a flashpoint when Trusty blocked Youssef Chermiti's strike with an arm. But it was tucked into his body and video assistant Andrew Dallas did not summon referee Don Robertson to the monitor.

Rangers were well on top in the opening quarter but that was the only real scare Celtic had and the visitors grew into the game.

Image: Rangers' Emmanuel Fernandez scored before it was ruled out for a handball

The away fans were celebrating 10 minutes from half-time when Maeda netted with a diving header but Scales was ultimately ruled offside by VAR after heading on McCowan's cross. It was one of the few times that Celtic threatened in the match.

Dujon Sterling and Chermiti looped efforts over before the break and Rangers spent most of the second period in the Celtic half.

Hoops goalkeeper Viljami Sinisalo stood up well to the aerial pressure and came off his line quickly to foil Chermiti following a mistake from Benjamin Arthur. The visitors needed some last-ditch blocks from Trusty and Sebastian Tounekti in particular to keep the scores blank.

Image: Celtic's Tomas Cvancara scores the winning penalty against Rangers

Rangers' physical advantage was forcing Celtic back and there was little respite for the visitors, who ended the 90 minutes with an all-new front three of Joel Mvuka, Cvancara and James Forrest.

The pattern continued into extra-time. Substitute Dane Murray's block denied Chermiti and Rangers then had a goal disallowed by VAR after the ball went into the net off the hand of Emmanuel Fernandez following a corner.

Image: Rangers' James Tavernier (C) and team-mates watch on during the penalty shoot-out

Chermiti volleyed wide from a good chance and Sinisalo saved from Nedim Bajrami as the action continued to flow one way, with Rangers ending the 120 minutes with 24 shots to Celtic's one.

Rangers won the toss for the spot-kicks but could not take advantage in front of the Copland Road Stand.

Rangers captain Tavernier went up first, but his effort smacked the crossbar and flew over.

Goalkeeper Jack Butland, who had saved eight of his previous nine penalties couldn't get near any of Celtic's four expertly dispatched kicks.

Gassama also saw his effort go over the top as Cvancara kept his goal to win the shoot-out 4-2 and spark chaos inside Ibrox.

Celtic supporters came onto the pitch to celebrate the result before a counter charge came from the home support.

Police and security intervened as the players were escorted off the field of play while both sets of fans launched missiles and pyrotechnics at one another.

The ugly scenes have since been condemned by the Scottish FA, who have vowed to carry out an investigation in line with Judicial Panel Protocol.

O'Neill: It was a proper Old Firm game

Celtic boss Martin O'Neill speaking in his press conference on whether the pitch invasion overshadowed the win:

"That would be a worry for me. The fixture itself, although still a phenomenal fixture, had lost a bit of its lustre because the away fans or full complement of away fans were not allowed in.

"We have no idea what might happen. I think today, with the crowd and our crowd there, to me, it was a proper Old Firm game in that sense. We will see what develops.

Image: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill looks on against Rangers

"It's a big derby game and I think there was a lot of jubilation at us winning. The fans spilt over and I suppose it is very difficult to quell that, I don't know.

"As we were making our way back, there was some kind of fracas and someone had tried to get on the field. I don't know, all I saw was a bit of a scrum down at the bottom.

"As you say, maybe it does taint proceedings but I think there was an element of self-protection in that one. I am making judgements on things I only half saw. From my viewpoint, it was a great win for us. Rangers are very strong and had the full week to go at it."

Röhl: Nobody wants these scenes

Rangers boss Danny Röhl speaking in his press conference about the pitch invasion:

"I was not on the pitch in the moment, I did not see it until now.

Image: Rangers boss Danny Röhl looks dejected as his side are knocked out of the Scottish Cup

"I think we all know the emotional situation after a game. Nobody likes to see this.

"It was a great atmosphere for 120 minutes and should be like this, the other things should not be in the stadium or in football."

Football.title: Eubank-Benn, Wardley, Usyk & Itauma all winners at Boxing Board awards
Football.content:
Conor Benn’s first fight with Chris Eubank Jr won the Contest of the Year prize at the prestigious British Boxing Board of Control awards.

New WBO heavyweight world champion Fabio Wardley won the award for Boxer of the Year and rising heavyweight star Moses Itauma received the Boxing Writers Young Boxer of Year Award.

Benn won his rematch with Eubank Jr last year as well, but lost to him in April when they headlined the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium for the first time.

That first fight was a 12-round thriller, made all the more spectacular by the surprise appearance of Chris Eubank Sr, the great rival of Conor's father Nigel Benn.

Benn has had tumultuous relations with the British Boxing Board of Control, which didn't sanction him to box in the UK until his provisional suspension, for his 2022 anti-doping violation, was lifted.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Winner of the Young Fighter of the Year at the British Boxing Board of Control Award Moses Itauma believes he is one of the best heavyweights in the world.

This prize will have personal significance to him - his father was the Board's 1995 Boxer of the Year. "You never know you have it in you until you have to prove it, so for me to be able to prove it as a silver spoon kid. More importantly we delivered what we said we were going to deliver," he said. "I'm glad everybody enjoyed it.

"I'm glad we did what we said we were going to do. It took a part of our souls that fight, we gave everything we had. I'm glad you guys were entertained. Ultimately it's the entertainment business.

"Hopefully there's plenty more nights like that. We're just getting started.

"Winning a world title will top that night and what that night meant for me to deliver."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of Joseph Parker’s heavyweight clash with Fabio Wardley at The O2 arena. (Credit: DAZN)

He added: "Ultimately it was a fight that will live with me forever. It took two to tango and Chris brought it out of me.

"We're not friends, he gave me one hell of a fight and only person could have brought that out of me."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports speak to BBBofC fighter of the year Fabio Wardley over his favourite moment of 2025 and his future plans!

Fabio Wardley's stellar year in 2025 saw him score a stunning late knockout to defeat Justis Huni at Portman Road in his Ipswich hometown.

He then came through a battle with Joseph Parker at the O2 Arena, eventually taking out the former world champion for another high level stoppage win. The WBO Interim title he won that night was upgraded to full world championship when Oleksandr Usyk vacated the belt.

Usyk was honoured at the ceremony. He won the Overseas Boxer of the Year award. In his only bout of 2025 Usyk knocked out Daniel Dubois at Wembley Stadium to become a two-time undisputed heavyweight world champion.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at the highlights from Oleksandr Usyk's spectacular knockout victory against Daniel Dubois at Wembley Stadium.

Young Boxer of the Year Itauma was only kept in the ring for three rounds last year, so good was he in knocking out Mike Balogun and former world title challenger Dillian Whyte.

Former undisputed world champion, Britain's first in the four-belt era, Josh Taylor, who retired last year after an unprecedented run of success, received a Special Recognition Award from the Board.

Three legendary editors of Boxing News, the iconic magazine that has been running since 1909, Claude Abrams, Tris Dixon and Matt Christie also received special awards.

The Boxing News Trainer of the Year prize went to Ben Davison, who coaches both Itauma and Wardley among others.

Dalton Smith, now the WBC super-lightweight world champion, won the Dennie Mancini Award for former or current Commonwealth champions.

Nigel Travis, who as well as coaching professional fighters runs a fantastic amateur boxing club for the community, and especially young people, in Moss Side, received the Services to Boxing Award.

Football.title: Port Vale beat Sunderland in almighty FA Cup shock
Football.content:
Port Vale produced one of the great FA Cup shocks as the League One basement side stunned Premier League Sunderland 1-0 to book their place in the quarter-finals.

It was a tie that looked like a formality on paper for the Black Cats - bottom of the third tier versus top flight - but the FA Cup worked its magic once again as Ben Waine's first-half header proved to be enough for Vale.

Waine - a Newcastle fan - pounced when Vale kept a corner alive and he looped a header home to send Vale Park into delirium. Five days after scoring the goal that knocked out Bristol City, Waine - a New Zealand international whose mother's family hails from the north east - was at it again to give Vale their first win over top-flight opposition since a fourth-round victory over Everton 30 years ago.

It is their first time in the quarter-finals of the FA Cup in more than 70 years since they made the last eight in 1954.

From the moment of taking the lead, it was backs-to-the-wall stuff for the team 11 points adrift in League One, but Sunderland did not do enough as Vale's defensive doggedness laid the platform for a memorable occasion.

Sunderland made only two changes from the side that beat Leeds in midweek to move up to 11th in the Premier League, but a side featuring more than £150m worth of talent blew the opportunity of winning some silverware.

The visitors could have led four minutes in when Vale goalkeeper Joe Gauci inadvertently punched a corner back across his own goal, but Eliezer Mayenda's header came back off a post before Kyle John scrambled it behind.

Sunderland nerves were laid bare when Waine forced Luke O'Nien into a long-range back-pass that left goalkeeper Melker Ellborg scrambling before he made an excellent if unorthodox save by heading over his own crossbar. It was from that mistake that Vale grabbed the lead through Waine via a corner.

Image: Waine heads Port Vale into a shock lead

Sunderland kept pushing, with Gauci blocking Nilson Angulo's header and then tipping over Dan Ballard's effort, but there was no denying Vale a day to savour.

Bottom of League One they might be, but yet again Port Vale reminded everyone why the FA Cup remains football's greatest stage for the improbable.

The FA Cup quarter-final draw is on Monday and will take place before the final last-16 tie between West Ham and Brentford.

Brady: I'm in a state of shock

Port Vale manager Jon Brady to TNT Sports:

"In a bit of shock really, to be honest. We worked out a game plan, worked very hard, we knew we would suffer without the ball and the pitch would help us.

"We've got a hell of a schedule. Over a quarter of the season with eight weeks left. Our top goalscorer was taken and sold to Luton, another boy goes to Plymouth and 55 per cent of our goals and assists went away from us. We have injuries in the backline and I have the smallest guy in the middle. But the last six games we have done well, we're undefeated.

"I asked Granit Xhaka, have you ever played on a pitch worse than this before? He said yes, I've played on worse!

"It hasn't sunk in really. I'm in a little bit of shock of the result. Not because I didn't think we'd do it, but you need a lot of things to go your way. When we first came in, the team was getting booed off at times, then we turned it round. The referee was starting to get booed at half-time and the fans were getting behind us. The connection with the fans, that's the most important thing.

Image: Waine celebrates his goal

"Six wins in 14 games for us since we've been here. It's about improving the mindset of the players. We've done a lot of things off the pitch to get them believing in themselves. Some of their journeys have been really raw, that's connected the players together.

"The fans have been through a lot. Our owner Carol (Shanahan), she is one of the most amazing ladies in business I've ever met. She said it's a game of snakes or ladders. If you go down, it's big snakes. If you go up, big ladders. That was a great analogy from her today."

Football.title: Jarvis misses out on DP World Tour history as Bradbury grabs Joburg Open win
Football.content:
Casey Jarvis narrowly missed out on matching a DP World Tour record as England's Dan Bradbury snatched a one-shot victory at the Joburg Open in South Africa.

Jarvis had won the Magical Kenya Open and the Investec South African Open Championship over the previous two weeks, leaving him looking to join Sir Nick Faldo and Seve Ballesteros as the only players to win three consecutive DP World Tour events.

The South African went into the final day two strokes back at Houghton GC but found himself top of the leaderboard with two holes to play, only to bogey the penultimate hole and fall short of a historic home win.

Image: Casey Jarvis narrowly missed out on a third consecutive DP World Tour win

Jarvis mixed six birdies with two bogeys to card a final-round 66 and finish tied-second on 16 under with England's Brandon Robinson Thompson, as Bradbury claimed his third DP World Tour win.

"I thought I'd lost it on the front nine," Bradbury said. "I had so many chances and just didn't take anything, but it feels really good to put it together on the back nine there. It feels amazing!"

Jarvis misses out as Bradbury claims Joburg title

Bradbury had also started two off the pace and was level par after seven holes of his final round, only to post a run of three consecutive birdies around the turn and add another at the par-three 14th.

Overnight-leader Hennie du Plessis dropped four shots in a five-hole stretch from the 12th and found himself three behind when Bradbury - also playing in the final threeball - birdied the par-four 17th to move to 17 under.

Jarvis - playing in the penultimate group - had bogeyed the same hole and failed to find a birdie at the last, while Robinson Thompson made two bogeys over the closing four holes - including a three-putt at the 18th - to also slip back to 16 under.

As Bradbury's title rivals all faded over the closing stretch, the Englishman produced a brilliant up and down from just off the 18th green to set up a tap-in par and claim the Joburg Open title for a second time.

"Casey ​and Brandon obviously played amazing - I saw their names on the leaderboard all the way around," Bradbury added. "It was pretty ⁠tight but I somehow managed to get it done in the end."
